title: Dockerfile Frontend, Build Context, Instructions, and Process Contracts · Runtime and Build Recall
summary: An operations reviewer reconstructs dockerfile frontend, build context, instructions, and process contracts from delayed evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and extends the reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract under a changed constraint. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.
Define local, Git, named, empty, and remote build contexts and use Dockerfile-specific ignore rules to minimize authority and invalidation.
Use FROM, WORKDIR, COPY, and justified ADD behavior with explicit ownership, paths, checksums, and context boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract
steps.instruction:
review case — A review build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts. Competency probe: Compose ENTRYPOINT and CMD into an override-friendly PID 1 contract and test arguments, signals, and exit status.

Inspect the Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compose entrypoint and cmd into an override-friendly pid 1 contract and test arguments, signals, and exit status. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract, keep Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Redesign context and runtime contracts. Competency probe: Compose ENTRYPOINT and CMD into an override-friendly PID 1 contract and test arguments, signals, and exit status.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: Multiple CMD instructions combine into a sequence of default commands.
